← all tutorials

Circuit Breaker: CLOSED → OPEN → HALF_OPEN — interactive CIRCUIT_BREAKER.md circuit_breaker.py

A circuit breaker is a fuse for your code: normally requests pass through (CLOSED); when the downstream fails threshold times in a row the fuse blows (OPEN) and every call fast-fails in ~1ms using no thread; after a cool-down it cautiously re-probes (HALF_OPEN). Drag the sliders — the 18-event trace and the worker-pool simulation recompute live in JS from the identical logic as circuit_breaker.py.  |  how failures are detected first →

① The state machine — drag the knobs, watch the 18-event trace replay

What to do: lower threshold and the breaker trips earlier; lower reset timeout and it re-probes sooner; raise half-open max and recovery needs more consecutive successes. The ticks re-run CANONICAL_TRACE live. Each tick is one request: ● admitted-success, ● admitted-failure, ● rejected (fast-fail).
replays CANONICAL_TRACE from circuit_breaker.py Sections A/B/C

② Cascade prevention — a 4-thread pool under a degraded downstream, with vs without a breaker

What this shows: each slow call (2000ms, failing) holds a worker thread for its full latency. Without a breaker (top lane) the pool fills up at 800ms and every later caller QUEUES — the slowdown cascades upstream. With a breaker (bottom lane) only the 3 calls in flight when it tripped ever touch the pool; the rest are rejected in-thread and use no worker, so worker 3 stays idle and nobody queues.
16 requests, 200ms apart, all failing. Breaker: threshold=3, timeout=5s.
worker busy on a slow downstream call caller queued (cascade — waiting for a worker) worker idle / request rejected in-thread

③ Config trade-offs — sensitive vs balanced vs tolerant on a failure burst

What to do: same FLAP_TRACE (a 4-failure burst then recovery) under three configs. Sensitive trips fast but false-trips on jitter; balanced is the Nygard/Hystrix default; tolerant never trips here — so it would offer no protection during a real outage. Numbers recompute from the live breaker.
check: … JS breaker replay matches circuit_breaker.py GOLD (18-event state sequence, 8 transitions, final state CLOSED).
